name: Greenwich LifeSciences, Inc.
business: Greenwich LifeSciences is a clinical-stage biopharmaceutical company focused on developing GLSI-100, an immunotherapy combining GP2 peptide and GM-CSF, to prevent breast cancer recurrences in HER2/neu positive patients. The company is currently conducting a Phase III clinical trial, Flamingo-01, evaluating GLSI-100 in patients with high-risk residual disease or pathologic complete response after trastuzumab-based treatment.
sellsTo: HER2/neu positive breast cancer patients with residual disease or high-risk pathologic complete response

exposures.sentence: We are a clinical-stage biopharmaceutical company focused on our Phase III clinical trial, Flamingo-01, which is evaluating GLSI-100, an immunotherapy to prevent breast cancer recurrences.

tamClaims.claim: The American Cancer Society estimates that approximately 1 in 8 U.S. women (12.8%) will develop invasive breast cancer over her lifetime.
tamClaims.sentence: The American Cancer Society estimates that approximately 1 in 8 U.S. women (12.8%) will develop invasive breast cancer over her lifetime.

tamClaims.claim: The American Cancer Society, Economic Impact, & European Cancer Information System 2025 estimate approximately 700,000 new breast cancer patients per year and 9.5 million current breast cancer survivors in the U.S. and Europe in 2025.
tamClaims.sentence: The American Cancer Society, Economic Impact, & European Cancer Information System 2025 estimate approximately 700,000 new breast cancer patients per year and 9.5 million current breast cancer survivors in the U.S. and Europe in 2025.

tamClaims.claim: Therefore, we believe that the U.S. market for the first indication for GP2, if approved, could be the combination of the three populations above which together comprises approximately 6.25% of breast cancer patients who undergo surgery.
tamClaims.sentence: Therefore, we believe that the U.S. market for the first indication for GP2, if approved, could be the combination of the three populations above which together comprises approximately 6.25% of breast cancer patients who undergo surgery.
